theory.intro: Parallel and perpendicular lines use symbols and diagram marks that communicate their relationship at a glance.
facts: $AB \parallel CD$ means line $AB$ is **parallel** to line $CD$; the lines remain the same distance apart.
$EF \perp GH$ means line $EF$ is **perpendicular** to line $GH$; they meet at $90^{\circ}$.
Matching arrowhead marks identify parallel lines. Different arrowhead counts identify different parallel sets.
A small square at an intersection is a right-angle mark and shows perpendicular lines.
A **transversal** is a line that intersects two or more other lines at different points.

foundation.question_text: Write using symbols: line $AB$ is parallel to line $CD$.
Write using symbols: line $PQ$ is perpendicular to line $RS$.

[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\draw (-2,1)--(2,1) node[right] {$B$};\node[left] at (-2,1) {$A$};\draw (-2,-1)--(2,-1) node[right] {$D$};\node[left] at (-2,-1) {$C$};\draw[->] (-0.5,1)--(0.25,1);\draw[->] (-0.5,-1)--(0.25,-1);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
What relationship is shown by the matching arrow marks?

[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\draw (-2,0)--(2,0) node[right] {$F$};\node[left] at (-2,0) {$E$};\draw (0,-1.5)--(0,1.5) node[above] {$H$};\node[below] at (0,-1.5) {$G$};\draw (0,0)--(0.28,0)--(0.28,0.28)--(0,0.28);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
What relationship is shown by the small square?

development.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\draw (-2,1)--(2,1) node[right] {$p$};\draw (-2,-1)--(2,-1) node[right] {$q$};\draw (-1.2,-1.7)--(0.7,1.7) node[above] {$t$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Line $t$ crosses lines $p$ and $q$ at different points. Name the transversal.

mastery.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.6]\draw (-2.5,1)--(2.5,1) node[right] {$p$};\draw (-2.5,-1)--(2.5,-1) node[right] {$q$};\draw (-1.8,-1.7)--(-0.6,1.7) node[above] {$r$};\draw (0.2,-1.7)--(1.5,1.7) node[above] {$s$};\draw (-2.4,0)--(2.4,0) node[right] {$u$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Which labelled lines are transversals of both $p$ and $q$?
